headline: Pharmaceutical Drug Prices 2.4 Times Higher Than International Reference
context: MOH study found medicine prices in Malaysia average 2.4 times the international reference price for essential drugs.

cards.big: Essential medicine prices in Malaysia are 2.4 times international reference benchmarks
cards.sub: Patients pay significantly more than necessary for life-saving medications

cards.big: Malaysia does not have a national drug pricing control mechanism for private sector
cards.sub: Pharmaceutical companies and pharmacies set prices based on market positioning

cards.big: Out-of-pocket pharmaceutical spending reached RM8.2 billion in 2025 or 38% of total OOP health costs
cards.sub: Medicine costs are the largest single driver of catastrophic health expenditure

cards.big: Bangladesh's essential drug pricing policy reduced medicine costs by 60% since implementation
cards.sub: Government reference pricing creates ceiling that prevents excessive markups

cards.big: When essential medicines cost 2.4 times what they should the market has failed patients
Should Malaysia implement mandatory reference pricing for all essential medicines
